87	    non-augmented concrete section Čech complex (with `Fp = (SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F`);
88	  - `ε : Fp.presheaf.obj (op V) ⟶ (sectionCechComplexV 𝒰 F V).X 0` is the augmentation map
89	    (the restriction `t ↦ (t|_{U'_i})_i`); and
90	  - `hε : ε ≫ (sectionCechComplexV 𝒰 F V).d 0 1 = 0`.
91	
92	Route (promote degreewise isos to a complex iso):
93	
94	(A) DEGREEWISE OBJECT ISO: `pushPull_eval_prod_iso` (Stub 4) gives, for each `p`,
95	    `D.X (p+1) ≅ (sectionCechComplexV 𝒰 F V).X p` as `Ab` objects — both equal `∏_σ Γ(U_σ ∩ V, F)`;
96	    and `D.X 0 = Fp.presheaf.obj (op V)` matches the augmentation object.
97	
98	(B) DIFFERENTIAL MATCH: The differential of `D'` is, read through `sectionCechProductEquiv`
99	    (`CechAcyclic.lean:1438`), the alternating sum `∑_i (-1)^i • sectionCechFaceRestr(σ,i)`
100	    (`sectionCech_objD_apply`, `CechAcyclic.lean:1513`). The differential of `D` is the
101	    evaluation-at-`V` of the Čech-nerve coface maps; under the degreewise identification
102	    (A), each coface of `D` becomes the corresponding face restriction of `D'`. REUSE
103	    `sectionCech_objD_apply` rather than rebuilding the alternating-sum bookkeeping.
104	
105	(C) ASSEMBLE: Build the `HomologicalComplex.mkIso` (or `HomologicalComplex.Hom` iso) from
106	    the degreewise components, checking the `comm` condition via the differential match.
107	
108	AMBIGUITY FLAG: The type of `Kp` in the definition of `D` uses
109	`PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)` as a technical adapter between
110	`SheafOfModules.forget` landing in `PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf.val` and the
111	`PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj` that the evaluation uses. The prover
112	should verify this adapter type carefully; if the exact path differs from the scaffold,
113	adjust `Kp` accordingly. Checking how `hSec` in `CechAugmentedResolution.lean:185-205`
114	constructs `Kp` provides the canonical reference.
115	
116	Key Lean names:
117	- `pushPull_eval_prod_iso` (Stub 4)
118	- `sectionCech_objD_apply` (CechAcyclic.lean:1513)
119	- `sectionCechProductEquiv` (CechAcyclic.lean:1438)
120	- `HomologicalComplex.mkIso` or `HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents`
121	
122	Difficulty: MEDIUM (assembly + differential bookkeeping via sectionCech_objD_apply). -/
123	noncomputable def cechSection_complex_iso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
124	    (F : X.Modules) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
125	    let α : X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ X.ringCatSheaf.obj := 𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj
126	    let cc := ComplexShape.up ℕ
127	    let K := cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F
128	    let Kp := ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
129	        PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj K
130	    let GV :=
131	      PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
132	      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op V)
133	    let D := (GV.mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj Kp
134	    D ≅ (sectionCechComplexV 𝒰 F V).augment (sectionCechAugV 𝒰 F V)
135	      (sectionCechAugV_comp_d 𝒰 F V) := by
136	  intro α cc K Kp GV D
137	  -- The push–pull functor `Ψ` through which the evaluated complex `D` is built.  We keep it
138	  -- inline (rather than abstracted by `set`) so the `Ψ.Additive` instance resolves directly.
139	  haveI hΨadd :
140	      (SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙ PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).Additive :=
141	    inferInstance
142	  haveI : GV.Additive := inferInstance
143	  -- (CORE, residual) Non-augmented degreewise iso + differential match: the evaluated
144	  -- non-augmented Čech complex `Γ(V, C•)` is the concrete section Čech complex over the
145	  -- restricted family `U'_σ = coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ⊓ V`.  Degreewise object iso is
146	  -- `pushPull_eval_prod_iso` (Stub 4); the differential match is via `sectionCech_objD_apply`.
147	  -- `coreIso` and `eY` are kept as `let`-bindings (transparent), so the degree-`0` identity
148	  -- `(isoApp coreIso 0).hom = (coreIso_objIso 𝒰 F 0 V).hom` and `eY.hom = 𝟙` hold definitionally,
149	  -- which is what makes `hcompat` close (the canonical `sectionCechAugV` is exactly the evaluated
150	  -- Čech augmentation transported across `coreIso_objIso 0`).
151	  let coreIso : (GV.mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj
152	        (((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
153	          PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj
154	            (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F)) ≅ sectionCechComplexV 𝒰 F V :=
155	    HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents (fun p => coreIso_objIso 𝒰 F p V)
156	      (coreIso_comm 𝒰 F V)
157	  -- (adapter) The augmentation node `GV(Ψ F)` is the section group `Γ(V, F)`: definitional,
158	  -- since `restrictScalars (𝟙 ·)` and `toPresheaf` leave the underlying abelian-group presheaf
159	  -- unchanged and evaluation extracts the section over `V`.
160	  let eY : GV.obj ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
161	        PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).obj F) ≅
162	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op V) := Iso.refl _
163	  -- (compat) The evaluated Čech augmentation equals the canonical `sectionCechAugV` read through
164	  -- `coreIso_objIso 0`; definitional, since `sectionCechAugV` is by construction
165	  -- `GV(Ψ(cechAugmentation)) ≫ (coreIso_objIso 𝒰 F 0 V).hom` and `eY.hom = 𝟙`.
166	  have hcompat : GV.map ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
167	        PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).map (cechAugmentation 𝒰 F)) ≫
168	        (HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoApp coreIso 0).hom = eY.hom ≫ sectionCechAugV 𝒰 F V := by
169	    have happ : (HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoApp coreIso 0).hom = (coreIso_objIso 𝒰 F 0 V).hom :=
170	      congrArg Iso.hom (HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents_app _ _ 0)
171	    rw [happ, sectionCechAugV]
172	    exact (Category.id_comp _).symm
173	  -- Peel the augmentation node off `D` with `mapHC_augment_iso` (twice), then glue the
174	  -- non-augmented `coreIso` to the augmentation data with `augmentCochainIso`.
175	  exact (GV.mapHomologicalComplex cc).mapIso
176	      (mapHC_augment_iso (SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
177	          PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α) hΨadd (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F) (cechAugmentation 𝒰 F)
178	        (cechAugmentation_comp_d 𝒰 F)) ≪≫
179	    mapHC_augment_iso GV ‹GV.Additive› (((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
180	        PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F))
181	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
182	        PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).map (cechAugmentation 𝒰 F))
183	      (map_augment_cond (SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
184	        PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α) hΨadd (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F) (cechAugmentation 𝒰 F)
185	        (cechAugmentation_comp_d 𝒰 F)) ≪≫
186	    augmentCochainIso coreIso eY (GV.map ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
187	        PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).map (cechAugmentation 𝒰 F)))
188	      (map_augment_cond GV ‹GV.Additive› (((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
189	          PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F))
190	        ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
191	          PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).map (cechAugmentation 𝒰 F))
192	        (map_augment_cond (SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
193	          PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α) hΨadd (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F) (cechAugmentation 𝒰 F)
194	          (cechAugmentation_comp_d 𝒰 F))) (sectionCechAugV 𝒰 F V)
195	      (sectionCechAugV_comp_d 𝒰 F V) hcompat
196	
197	/-! ## Stub 6 — Contracting homotopy on the augmented concrete section Čech complex -/
198	
199	/-! ### Engine instantiation for Stub 6.
200	
201	The dependent-coefficient combinatorial Čech engine (`CombinatorialCech.depHomotopy_spec`,
202	CechAcyclic.lean) is instantiated with the augmentation node `Γ(V, F)` as level `0` and the
203	restricted Čech coefficients `Γ(⨅ₖ (U_{σ k} ⊓ V), F)` as levels `≥ 1`.  The coface maps `δ`
204	are the presheaf face restrictions (level `0 → 1` is the augmentation restriction), and the
205	prepend maps `c` are genuine restrictions because `V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i_fix` forces
206	`U'_{i_fix·σ} = U'_σ`.  The `hu`/`hsh` compatibilities collapse to "two parallel restriction
207	chains between the same opens agree". -/
208	
209	section Stub6Engine
210	
211	variable (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
212	
213	/-- Composite of two presheaf restrictions is the direct restriction (local copy of the
214	`CechBridge` private helper `restr_trans`). -/
215	private lemma stubRestrTrans (P : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab.{u})
216	    {A B C : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X} (h1 : A ≤ B) (h2 : B ≤ C)
217	    (x : ToType (P.obj (Opposite.op C))) :
218	    ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE h1).op)
219	        (ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE h2).op) x)
220	      = ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE (h1.trans h2)).op) x := by
221	  rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← P.map_comp, ← op_comp]
222	  rfl
223	
224	/-- Two parallel presheaf restrictions agree (poset-hom uniqueness; local copy of the
225	`CechBridge` private helper `restr_op_unique`). -/
226	private lemma stubRestrUnique (P : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab.{u})
227	    {A C : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X} (f g : Opposite.op C ⟶ Opposite.op A)
228	    (x : ToType (P.obj (Opposite.op C))) :
229	    ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map f) x = ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map g) x := by
230	  rw [show f = g from Quiver.Hom.unop_inj (Subsingleton.elim _ _)]
231	
232	/-- A nonempty restricted Čech intersection is contained in `V`. -/
233	private lemma stubInterLeV {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → 𝒰.I₀) :
234	    (⨅ k, (coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k) ⊓ V)) ≤ V :=
235	  le_trans (iInf_le _ 0) inf_le_right
236	
237	/-- Prepending `i_fix` does not shrink the restricted intersection (positive levels). -/
238	private lemma stubConsLe {m : ℕ} (i_fix : 𝒰.I₀) (hiV : V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i_fix)
239	    (σ : Fin (m + 1) → 𝒰.I₀) :
240	    (⨅ k, (coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k) ⊓ V)) ≤
241	      ⨅ k, (coverOpen 𝒰 ((Fin.cons i_fix σ : Fin (m + 2) → 𝒰.I₀) k) ⊓ V) := by
242	  refine le_iInf fun k => ?_
243	  refine Fin.cases ?_ ?_ k
244	  · rw [Fin.cons_zero]
245	    exact le_trans (stubInterLeV 𝒰 V σ) (le_inf hiV le_rfl)
246	  · intro j
247	    rw [Fin.cons_succ]
248	    exact iInf_le _ j
249	
250	/-- Prepending `i_fix` to the empty tuple yields an intersection containing `V`
251	(the augmentation node case). -/
252	private lemma stubConsLeZero (i_fix : 𝒰.I₀) (hiV : V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i_fix)
253	    (σ : Fin 0 → 𝒰.I₀) :
254	    V ≤ ⨅ k, (coverOpen 𝒰 ((Fin.cons i_fix σ : Fin 1 → 𝒰.I₀) k) ⊓ V) := by
255	  refine le_iInf fun k => ?_
256	  rw [Fin.fin_one_eq_zero k, Fin.cons_zero]
257	  exact le_inf hiV le_rfl
258	
259	/-- The open underlying the level-`m` Stub-6 coefficient: level `0` is `V` (the
260	augmentation node), level `m+1` is the restricted intersection `⨅ₖ (U_{σ k} ⊓ V)`. -/
261	private def stubOpen : (m : ℕ) → (Fin m → 𝒰.I₀) → TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X
262	  | 0, _ => V
263	  | _ + 1, σ => ⨅ k, (coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k) ⊓ V)
264	
265	/-- The coface inclusion of the Stub-6 opens. -/
266	private lemma stubOpen_le_coface : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → 𝒰.I₀) (j : Fin (m + 1)),
267	    stubOpen 𝒰 V (m + 1) σ ≤ stubOpen 𝒰 V m (σ ∘ j.succAbove)
268	  | 0, σ, _ => stubInterLeV 𝒰 V σ
269	  | _ + 1, σ, j => le_iInf fun l => iInf_le _ (j.succAbove l)
270	
271	/-- The prepend inclusion of the Stub-6 opens (prepending `i_fix` does not shrink the
272	open, because `V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i_fix`). -/
273	private lemma stubOpen_le_prepend (i_fix : 𝒰.I₀) (hiV : V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i_fix) :
274	    ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin m → 𝒰.I₀),
275	      stubOpen 𝒰 V m σ ≤ stubOpen 𝒰 V (m + 1) (Fin.cons i_fix σ)
276	  | 0, σ => stubConsLeZero 𝒰 V i_fix hiV σ
277	  | _ + 1, σ => stubConsLe 𝒰 V i_fix hiV σ
278	
279	/-- Dependent coefficient family for the Stub-6 engine: the sections of `F` over
280	`stubOpen m σ`.  Kept as a reducible abbreviation so the `AddCommGroup` instance is the
281	generic one on `Ab`-objects (no bespoke match-instance). -/
282	private noncomputable abbrev cechSectionCoeff (m : ℕ) (σ : Fin m → 𝒰.I₀) : Type u :=
283	  ToType (((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.obj
284	    (Opposite.op (stubOpen 𝒰 V m σ)))
285	
286	/-- The engine coface maps: presheaf face restrictions (level `0 → 1` is the augmentation
287	restriction `Γ(V) → Γ(U'_σ)`). -/
288	private noncomputable def cechSectionCoface (m : ℕ) (σ : Fin (m + 1) → 𝒰.I₀)
289	    (j : Fin (m + 1)) :
290	    cechSectionCoeff 𝒰 F V m (σ ∘ j.succAbove) →+ cechSectionCoeff 𝒰 F V (m + 1) σ :=
291	  ConcreteCategory.hom (((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.map
292	    (homOfLE (stubOpen_le_coface 𝒰 V σ j)).op)
293	
294	/-- The engine prepend maps: genuine restrictions (level `1 → 0` is the restriction
295	`Γ(U'_{(i_fix)}) → Γ(V)` along `V ≤ U'_{i_fix}`). -/
296	private noncomputable def cechSectionPrepend (i_fix : 𝒰.I₀)
297	    (hiV : V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i_fix) (m : ℕ) (σ : Fin m → 𝒰.I₀) :
298	    cechSectionCoeff 𝒰 F V (m + 1) (Fin.cons i_fix σ) →+ cechSectionCoeff 𝒰 F V m σ :=
299	  ConcreteCategory.hom (((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.map
300	    (homOfLE (stubOpen_le_prepend 𝒰 V i_fix hiV σ)).op)
301	
302	/-- Transport of a Čech coefficient along an equality of index tuples is the canonical
303	restriction between the (equal) intersection opens. -/
304	private lemma cechSectionCoeff_transport {m : ℕ} {τ₁ τ₂ : Fin (m + 1) → 𝒰.I₀}
305	    (h : τ₁ = τ₂)
306	    (hle : stubOpen 𝒰 V (m + 1) τ₂ ≤ stubOpen 𝒰 V (m + 1) τ₁)
307	    (y : cechSectionCoeff 𝒰 F V (m + 1) τ₁) :
308	    (h ▸ y : cechSectionCoeff 𝒰 F V (m + 1) τ₂)
309	      = ConcreteCategory.hom
310	          (((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.map (homOfLE hle).op)
311	          y := by
312	  subst h
313	  rw [show homOfLE hle = 𝟙 _ from Subsingleton.elim _ _, op_id,
314	    CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
315	  rfl
316	
317	/-- Unit compatibility `hu` for the Stub-6 engine: deleting the prepended `i_fix` and then
318	applying the prepend restriction is the identity transport (both sides are restriction
319	chains between the same opens). -/
320	private lemma cechSection_hu (i_fix : 𝒰.I₀) (hiV : V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i_fix)
321	    {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → 𝒰.I₀)
322	    (y : cechSectionCoeff 𝒰 F V (m + 1)
323	      ((Fin.cons i_fix σ : Fin (m + 2) → 𝒰.I₀) ∘ (0 : Fin (m + 2)).succAbove)) :
324	    cechSectionPrepend 𝒰 F V i_fix hiV (m + 1) σ
325	        (cechSectionCoface 𝒰 F V (m + 1) (Fin.cons i_fix σ) 0 y)
326	      = (CombinatorialCech.cons_comp_zero_succAbove i_fix σ) ▸ y := by
327	  rw [cechSectionCoeff_transport 𝒰 F V (CombinatorialCech.cons_comp_zero_succAbove i_fix σ)
328	    (le_of_eq (by rw [CombinatorialCech.cons_comp_zero_succAbove i_fix σ]))]
329	  exact (stubRestrTrans _ _ _ y).trans (stubRestrUnique _ _ _ y)
330	
331	/-- Shift compatibility `hsh` for the Stub-6 engine: prepend commutes with the later
332	cofaces (both sides are restriction chains between the same opens). -/
333	private lemma cechSection_hsh (i_fix : 𝒰.I₀) (hiV : V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i_fix)
334	    {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → 𝒰.I₀) (k : Fin (m + 1))
335	    (y : cechSectionCoeff 𝒰 F V (m + 1)
336	      ((Fin.cons i_fix σ : Fin (m + 2) → 𝒰.I₀) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove)) :
337	    cechSectionPrepend 𝒰 F V i_fix hiV (m + 1) σ
338	        (cechSectionCoface 𝒰 F V (m + 1) (Fin.cons i_fix σ) k.succ y)
339	      = cechSectionCoface 𝒰 F V m σ k
340	          (cechSectionPrepend 𝒰 F V i_fix hiV m (σ ∘ k.succAbove)
341	            ((CombinatorialCech.cons_comp_succAbove_succ i_fix σ k) ▸ y)) := by
342	  have hle : stubOpen 𝒰 V (m + 1) (Fin.cons i_fix (σ ∘ k.succAbove)) ≤
343	      stubOpen 𝒰 V (m + 1) ((Fin.cons i_fix σ : Fin (m + 2) → 𝒰.I₀) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove) :=
344	    le_of_eq (by rw [CombinatorialCech.cons_comp_succAbove_succ i_fix σ k])
345	  rw [cechSectionCoeff_transport 𝒰 F V
346	    (CombinatorialCech.cons_comp_succAbove_succ i_fix σ k) hle]
347	  refine Eq.trans (stubRestrTrans _ (stubOpen_le_prepend 𝒰 V i_fix hiV σ)
348	    (stubOpen_le_coface 𝒰 V (Fin.cons i_fix σ) k.succ) y) ?_
349	  refine Eq.trans (stubRestrUnique _ _ (homOfLE ((stubOpen_le_coface 𝒰 V σ k).trans
350	    ((stubOpen_le_prepend 𝒰 V i_fix hiV (σ ∘ k.succAbove)).trans hle))).op y) ?_
351	  refine Eq.symm ?_
352	  refine Eq.trans (DFunLike.congr_arg (cechSectionCoface 𝒰 F V m σ k)
353	    (stubRestrTrans _ (stubOpen_le_prepend 𝒰 V i_fix hiV (σ ∘ k.succAbove)) hle y)) ?_
354	  exact stubRestrTrans _ (stubOpen_le_coface 𝒰 V σ k)
355	    ((stubOpen_le_prepend 𝒰 V i_fix hiV (σ ∘ k.succAbove)).trans hle) y
356	
357	end Stub6Engine
358	
359	/-! ### Degree-0 augmentation seam — helper bricks for `sectionCechAugV_π`.
360	
361	The seam unwinds `sectionCechAugV ≫ Pi.π σ` through the proved Base seams.  The Čech
362	augmentation composed with the push–pull of *any* `Over X`-morphism `g : Y ⟶ Y₀` into the
363	backbone collapses — through the terminal object `Over.mk (𝟙 X)` of `Over X` — to the
364	pullback–pushforward adjunction unit at `Y.hom` (`cechAugmentation_pushPullMap`); the unit, in
365	turn, reads through the per-leg section identification `pushPull_leg_sections` as the plain
366	presheaf restriction (`unit_pushPull_leg_sections`), because it is conjugate via
367	`Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq` to the restriction adjunction whose unit *is* the restriction on
368	sections (`Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction_unit_app_app`). -/
369	
370	section AugSeam
371	
372	/-- An `eqToHom` between section groups of an abelian presheaf over (propositionally) equal
373	opens is the presheaf restriction along the induced inclusion.  Transport-killer for the
374	augmentation seam. -/
375	private lemma stubEqToHomRestr (P : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab.{u})
376	    {A B : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X} (hAB : A = B) (hle : B ≤ A)
377	    (h : P.obj (Opposite.op A) = P.obj (Opposite.op B)) :
378	    eqToHom h = P.map (homOfLE hle).op := by
379	  subst hAB
380	  refine Eq.trans (eqToHom_refl _ h) ?_
381	  refine Eq.trans (P.map_id (Opposite.op A)).symm ?_
382	  exact congrArg P.map (congrArg Quiver.Hom.op (Subsingleton.elim (𝟙 A) (homOfLE hle)))
383	
384	/-- Precomposing the scheme-level push–pull comparison `rawPushPullMap` with the
385	pullback–pushforward adjunction unit at the source structure map yields the unit at the
386	target structure map (`η^{p₁} ≫ G(a) = η^{p₂}`, the mate-calculus collapse of the degree-`0`
387	augmentation leg). -/
388	private lemma rawPushPullMap_unit {Z₁ Z₂ : Scheme.{u}} (a : Z₂ ⟶ Z₁)
389	    (p₁ : Z₁ ⟶ X) (p₂ : Z₂ ⟶ X) (w : a ≫ p₁ = p₂) (F : X.Modules) :
390	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p₁).unit.app F ≫
391	        rawPushPullMap a p₁ p₂ w F =
392	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p₂).unit.app F := by
393	  subst w
394	  rw [rawPushPullMap_self, pushPull_unit_comp a p₁ F]
395	  refine congrArg (fun m =>
396	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction p₁).unit.app F ≫ m) ?_
397	  refine Eq.trans ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₁).map_comp _ _) ?_
398	  refine congrArg (fun m => (Scheme.Modules.pushforward p₁).map
399	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction a).unit.app
400	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback p₁).obj F)) ≫ m) ?_
401	  -- `pushforward` is strict: `p₁_*(a_* χ) = (pushforwardComp).hom ≫ (a ≫ p₁)_* χ`
402	  -- (the comparison cell is the identity on sections).
403	  apply Scheme.Modules.hom_ext
404	  intro U
405	  rfl

407	/-- The inverse of the Čech-nerve point identification is the pullback–pushforward
408	adjunction unit of the identity morphism. -/
409	private lemma cechNervePointIso_inv_eq_unit (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
410	    (cechNervePointIso 𝒰 F).inv =
411	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (𝟙 X)).unit.app F := by
412	  have star := unit_conjugateEquiv (Adjunction.id (C := X.Modules))
413	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (𝟙 X)) (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X).hom F
414	  rw [Scheme.Modules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom] at star
415	  simp only [Adjunction.id_unit, NatTrans.id_app, Functor.id_obj] at star
416	  have star2 : (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId X).inv.app F =
417	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (𝟙 X)).unit.app F ≫
418	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (𝟙 X)).map ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X).hom.app F) :=
419	    (Category.id_comp _).symm.trans star
420	  have hnat := (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId X).inv.naturality
421	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X).inv.app F)
422	  simp only [Functor.id_obj, Functor.id_map] at hnat
423	  refine Eq.trans (hnat : (cechNervePointIso 𝒰 F).inv = _) ?_
424	  refine Eq.trans (congrArg (fun m => m ≫ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (𝟙 X)).map
425	    ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X).inv.app F)) star2) ?_
426	  refine Eq.trans (Category.assoc _ _ _) ?_
427	  refine Eq.trans (congrArg (fun m =>
428	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (𝟙 X)).unit.app F ≫ m)
429	    (((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (𝟙 X)).map_comp _ _).symm)) ?_
430	  refine Eq.trans (congrArg (fun m =>
431	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (𝟙 X)).unit.app F ≫
432	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (𝟙 X)).map m) (Iso.hom_inv_id_app _ _)) ?_
433	  exact (congrArg (fun m =>
434	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (𝟙 X)).unit.app F ≫ m)
435	      ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (𝟙 X)).map_id _)).trans (Category.comp_id _)

437	/-- The Čech augmentation composed with the push–pull of *any* `Over X`-morphism into the
438	degree-`0` backbone is the pullback–pushforward adjunction unit at the structure map: the
439	composite `Y ⟶ Y₀ ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X)` is a morphism into the terminal object, so the
440	augmentation leg needs no unwinding. -/
441	private lemma cechAugmentation_pushPullMap (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules)
442	    {Y : Over X}
443	    (g : Y ⟶ (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk 0))) :
444	    cechAugmentation 𝒰 F ≫ pushPullMap F g =
445	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction Y.hom).unit.app F := by
446	  have happ : (CechNerve 𝒰 F).hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 0) =
447	      pushPullMap F
448	        ((coverCechNerveOverAug 𝒰).hom.app (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk 0))) :=
449	    Eq.trans rfl (Category.id_comp _)
450	  rw [cechAugmentation, Category.assoc]
451	  refine Eq.trans (congrArg (fun m => (cechNervePointIso 𝒰 F).inv ≫ m)
452	    (Eq.trans (congrArg (fun m => m ≫ pushPullMap F g) happ)
453	      ((pushPullMap_comp F _ g).symm))) ?_
454	  rw [cechNervePointIso_inv_eq_unit, pushPullMap_eq_raw]
455	  exact rawPushPullMap_unit _ _ _ _ F

458	/-- The pullback–pushforward unit reads through the per-leg section identification as the
459	plain `F`-restriction `Γ(V, F) → Γ(U_σ ∩ V, F)`. -/
460	private lemma unit_pushPull_leg_sections (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (F : X.Modules)
461	    {p : ℕ} (σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
462	    (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X ⋙
463	        (CategoryTheory.evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ (Ab.{u})).obj
464	          (Opposite.op V)).map
465	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
466	          (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))).unit.app F) ≫
467	      (pushPull_leg_sections 𝒰 F σ V).hom =
468	    ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.map
469	      (homOfLE (inf_le_right : coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ⊓ V ≤ V)).op := by
470	  have hW : Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ) ''ᵁ
471	        (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ) ⁻¹ᵁ V) = coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ⊓ V := by
472	    rw [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf, Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι]
473	  -- (i) the leg iso, with its `eqToHom` transport canonicalised to the `congrArg` form
474	  -- (definitional: projections of `Iso.trans`/`eqToIso` plus proof irrelevance)
475	  have hdec : (pushPull_leg_sections 𝒰 F σ V).hom =
476	      (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X ⋙
477	          (CategoryTheory.evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ (Ab.{u})).obj
478	            (Opposite.op V)).map
479	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))).map
480	          ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback
481	            (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))).inv.app F)) ≫
482	      eqToHom (congrArg
483	        (fun W => ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op W))
484	        hW) := rfl
485	  -- (ii) the pullback unit is conjugate to the restriction unit via `leftAdjointUniq`
486	  -- (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback` *is* the `leftAdjointUniq` iso, definitionally)
487	  have hLAU : (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
488	        (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))).unit.app F ≫
489	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))).map
490	        ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback
491	          (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))).inv.app F) =
492	      (Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction
493	        (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))).unit.app F := by
494	    have h0 : (Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction
495	          (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))).unit.app F ≫
496	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))).map
497	          ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback
498	            (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))).hom.app F) =
499	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
500	          (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))).unit.app F :=
501	      Adjunction.unit_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app _ _ F
502	    refine Eq.trans (congrArg (fun m => m ≫
503	      (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))).map
504	        ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback
505	          (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))).inv.app F)) h0.symm) ?_
506	    refine Eq.trans (Category.assoc _ _ _) ?_
507	    refine Eq.trans (congrArg (fun m => (Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction
508	        (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))).unit.app F ≫ m)
509	      (((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))).map_comp
510	        _ _).symm)) ?_
511	    refine Eq.trans (congrArg (fun m => (Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction
512	        (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))).unit.app F ≫
513	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))).map m)
514	      (Iso.hom_inv_id_app _ _)) ?_
515	    exact (congrArg (fun m => (Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction
516	        (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))).unit.app F ≫ m)
517	      ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))).map_id _)).trans
518	      (Category.comp_id _)
519	  -- (iii) the restriction unit on sections is the plain restriction (definitional,
520	  -- `Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction_unit_app_app`)
521	  have hunit : (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X ⋙
522	        (CategoryTheory.evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ (Ab.{u})).obj
523	          (Opposite.op V)).map
524	      ((Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction
525	        (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))).unit.app F) =
526	    ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.map
527	      (homOfLE ((Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)).image_preimage_le V)).op := rfl
528	  -- assemble: fuse the unit with the comparison leg, read off the restriction, and
529	  -- collapse the two parallel restriction chains (term-chained: `rw` cannot re-match
530	  -- composites whose stored middle objects are defeq-but-not-syntactic).
531	  refine Eq.trans (congrArg (fun m => (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X ⋙
532	      (CategoryTheory.evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ (Ab.{u})).obj
533	        (Opposite.op V)).map
534	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction
535	        (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))).unit.app F) ≫ m) hdec) ?_
536	  refine Eq.trans (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm ?_
537	  refine Eq.trans (congrArg (fun m => m ≫ eqToHom (congrArg
538	      (fun W => ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op W))
539	      hW))
540	    (Eq.trans (((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X ⋙
541	        (CategoryTheory.evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ (Ab.{u})).obj
542	          (Opposite.op V)).map_comp _ _).symm)
543	      (Eq.trans (congrArg (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X ⋙
544	        (CategoryTheory.evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ (Ab.{u})).obj
545	          (Opposite.op V)).map hLAU) hunit))) ?_
546	  refine Eq.trans (congrArg (fun m =>
547	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.map
548	        (homOfLE ((Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)).image_preimage_le V)).op ≫ m)
549	    (stubEqToHomRestr (((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf) hW
550	      (le_of_eq hW.symm) _)) ?_
551	  refine Eq.trans
552	    ((((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.map_comp _ _).symm) ?_
553	  refine Eq.trans (congrArg ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.map
554	    (op_comp (f := homOfLE (le_of_eq hW.symm))
555	      (g := homOfLE ((Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)).image_preimage_le V))).symm) ?_
556	  exact congrArg (fun m => ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.map
557	    (Quiver.Hom.op m)) (Subsingleton.elim _ _)

563	-- raised: instance synthesis (`HasLimit`/`PreservesLimitsOfShape` over the heavy
564	-- push–pull product types) exceeds the default budget on this file.
565	/-- **Coordinatewise identification of the canonical augmentation** (the degree-`0`
566	augmentation seam of `lem:cechSection_contractible`): the `σ`-coordinate of
567	`sectionCechAugV` is the plain restriction `Γ(V, F) → Γ(U'_σ, F)`.  This is the `p = 0`
568	leg unwinding of `coreIso_objIso` — through `pushPull_sigma_iso`'s `σ`-leg, the terminal
569	object of `Over X` (which collapses `a₀(σ) ≫ (augmentation)` to the canonical map
570	`Over.mk j_σ ⟶ Over.mk (𝟙 X)` with NO unwinding of `a₀`), and the section computation of
571	the push–pull adjunction unit. -/
572	lemma sectionCechAugV_π (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (F : X.Modules)
573	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (σ : Fin 1 → 𝒰.I₀) :
574	    sectionCechAugV 𝒰 F V ≫ Pi.π (fun τ : Fin 1 → 𝒰.I₀ =>
575	        ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.obj
576	          (Opposite.op (⨅ l, (coverOpen 𝒰 (τ l) ⊓ V)))) σ =
577	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.map
578	        (homOfLE (stubInterLeV 𝒰 V σ)).op := by
579	  -- Instances for the evaluated-product seam (mirrors `pushPull_eval_prod_iso`).
580	  haveI hT : Limits.PreservesLimitsOfShape (Discrete (Fin (0 + 1) → 𝒰.I₀))
581	      (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X) := inferInstance
582	  haveI : Limits.HasLimitsOfShape (Discrete (Fin (0 + 1) → 𝒰.I₀)) (Ab.{u}) :=
583	    inferInstance
584	  haveI hE2 : Limits.PreservesLimitsOfShape (Discrete (Fin (0 + 1) → 𝒰.I₀))
585	      ((CategoryTheory.evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ (Ab.{u})).obj
586	        (Opposite.op V)) :=
587	    Limits.evaluation_preservesLimitsOfShape _
588	  haveI : Limits.PreservesLimitsOfShape (Discrete (Fin (0 + 1) → 𝒰.I₀))
589	      (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X ⋙
590	        (CategoryTheory.evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ (Ab.{u})).obj
591	          (Opposite.op V)) :=
592	    @Limits.comp_preservesLimitsOfShape _ _ _ _ (Discrete (Fin (0 + 1) → 𝒰.I₀)) _ _ _
593	      (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X)
594	      ((CategoryTheory.evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ (Ab.{u})).obj
595	        (Opposite.op V)) hT hE2
596	  -- (1) The degree-`0` object iso is `pushPull_eval_prod_iso` reindexed (definitional).
597	  have hcoreDef : (coreIso_objIso 𝒰 F 0 V).hom =
598	      (pushPull_eval_prod_iso 𝒰 F 0 V).hom ≫
599	        (Limits.Pi.mapIso (fun τ : Fin (0 + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
600	          eqToIso (congrArg
601	            (fun W => ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.obj
602	              (Opposite.op W))
603	            (coverInterOpen_inf_eq_iInf_inf 𝒰 τ V)))).hom := rfl
604	  -- (2) `pushPull_eval_prod_iso` decomposed into its three factors (definitional).
605	  have hevalDef : (pushPull_eval_prod_iso 𝒰 F 0 V).hom =
606	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X ⋙
607	          (CategoryTheory.evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ (Ab.{u})).obj
608	            (Opposite.op V)).mapIso (pushPull_sigma_iso 𝒰 F 0)).hom ≫
609	        (Limits.PreservesProduct.iso (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X ⋙
610	            (CategoryTheory.evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ (Ab.{u})).obj
611	              (Opposite.op V))
612	          (fun τ : Fin (0 + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
613	            pushPullObj F (Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ))))).hom ≫
614	        (Limits.Pi.mapIso (fun τ : Fin (0 + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
615	          pushPull_leg_sections 𝒰 F τ V)).hom := rfl
616	  -- (3) π-extraction through the reindexing `Pi.mapIso` (the `eqToIso` family).
617	  have hmapiso1 : (Limits.Pi.mapIso (fun τ : Fin (0 + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
618	        eqToIso (congrArg
619	          (fun W => ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.obj
620	            (Opposite.op W))
621	          (coverInterOpen_inf_eq_iInf_inf 𝒰 τ V)))).hom =
622	      Limits.Pi.map (fun τ : Fin (0 + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
623	        eqToHom (congrArg
624	          (fun W => ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.obj
625	            (Opposite.op W))
626	          (coverInterOpen_inf_eq_iInf_inf 𝒰 τ V))) := rfl
627	  -- (4) π-extraction through the per-leg `Pi.mapIso`.
628	  have hmapiso2 : (Limits.Pi.mapIso (fun τ : Fin (0 + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
629	        pushPull_leg_sections 𝒰 F τ V)).hom =
630	      Limits.Pi.map (fun τ : Fin (0 + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
631	        (pushPull_leg_sections 𝒰 F τ V).hom) := rfl
632	  -- (5) The evaluated `GV ∘ Ψ` augmentation is the evaluation of `cechAugmentation`
633	  -- (definitional: `restrictScalars (𝟙 ·)` and `toPresheaf` commute with `forget`).

635	        (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op V)).map
636	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
637	        PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).map
638	          (cechAugmentation 𝒰 F)) =
639	      (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X ⋙
640	        (CategoryTheory.evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ (Ab.{u})).obj
641	          (Opposite.op V)).map (cechAugmentation 𝒰 F) := rfl
642	  -- (6) Unwind `coreIso_objIso 0 ≫ Pi.π σ` down to the Base seams (term-chained:
643	  -- `rw` cannot re-match composites whose stored middle objects are

645	  have hproj : (coreIso_objIso 𝒰 F 0 V).hom ≫
646	      Pi.π (fun τ : Fin 1 → 𝒰.I₀ =>
647	        ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.obj
648	          (Opposite.op (⨅ l, (coverOpen 𝒰 (τ l) ⊓ V)))) σ =
649	      (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X ⋙
650	        (CategoryTheory.evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ (Ab.{u})).obj
651	          (Opposite.op V)).map
652	        (pushPullMap F (coprodOverIncl (fun τ : Fin (0 + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
653	            Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ))) σ ≫
654	          (overSigmaDescIso (fun τ : Fin (0 + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
655	            (Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ))).hom)).inv ≫
656	          (cechBackbone_left_sigma 𝒰 0).inv)) ≫
657	        (pushPull_leg_sections 𝒰 F σ V).hom ≫
658	        eqToHom (congrArg
659	          (fun W => ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.obj
660	            (Opposite.op W))
661	          (coverInterOpen_inf_eq_iInf_inf 𝒰 σ V)) := by
662	    refine Eq.trans (congrArg (fun m => m ≫ _) hcoreDef) ?_
663	    refine Eq.trans (Category.assoc _ _ _) ?_
664	    refine Eq.trans (congrArg (fun m => _ ≫ m)
665	      (Eq.trans (congrArg (fun m => m ≫ _) hmapiso1) (Limits.Pi.map_π _ σ))) ?_
666	    refine Eq.trans (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm ?_
667	    refine Eq.trans (congrArg (fun m => m ≫ _) (?_ :
668	      (pushPull_eval_prod_iso 𝒰 F 0 V).hom ≫
669	        Pi.π (fun τ : Fin (0 + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
670	          ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.obj
671	            (Opposite.op (coverInterOpen 𝒰 τ ⊓ V))) σ = _ ≫ _)) (Category.assoc _ _ _)
672	    refine Eq.trans (congrArg (fun m => m ≫ _) hevalDef) ?_
673	    refine Eq.trans (Category.assoc _ _ _) ?_
674	    refine Eq.trans (congrArg (fun m => _ ≫ m) (Category.assoc _ _ _)) ?_
675	    refine Eq.trans (congrArg (fun m => _ ≫ _ ≫ m)
676	      (Eq.trans (congrArg (fun m => m ≫ _) hmapiso2) (Limits.Pi.map_π _ σ))) ?_
677	    refine Eq.trans (congrArg (fun m => _ ≫ m) (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm) ?_
678	    refine Eq.trans (congrArg (fun m => _ ≫ (m ≫ _))
679	      (Eq.trans (congrArg (fun m => m ≫ _) (Limits.PreservesProduct.iso_hom _ _))
680	        (Limits.piComparison_comp_π _ _ _))) ?_
681	    refine Eq.trans (Category.assoc _ _ _).symm ?_
682	    refine congrArg (fun m => m ≫ (pushPull_leg_sections 𝒰 F σ V).hom) ?_
683	    refine Eq.trans (((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X ⋙
684	      (CategoryTheory.evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ (Ab.{u})).obj
685	        (Opposite.op V)).map_comp _ _).symm) ?_
686	    exact congrArg (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X ⋙
687	      (CategoryTheory.evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ (Ab.{u})).obj
688	        (Opposite.op V)).map (pushPull_sigma_iso_π 𝒰 F 0 σ)
689	  -- (7) Final assembly: the augmentation collapses through the terminal object to the
690	  -- adjunction unit, which reads as the plain restriction; the residual transports are
691	  -- parallel restriction chains between the same opens.
692	  refine Eq.trans (congrArg (fun m => m ≫ _) (rfl :
693	    sectionCechAugV 𝒰 F V =
694	      (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
695	          (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op V)).map
696	        ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙

10	/-!
11	# Sub-brick A: identifying the evaluated augmented Čech section complex
12	  (blueprint `lem:cech_backbone_left_sigma` … `lem:cechSection_contractible`)
13	
14	This file is the shared "Sub-brick A" chain that
15	
16	1. identifies the degree-`p` Čech-nerve backbone `(coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj [p]` with
17	   the coproduct `∐_σ Over.mk j_σ` in `Over X` (`cechBackbone_left_sigma`);
18	2. decomposes the push-pull object `pushPullObj F Y_p` as a product in `X.Modules`
19	   (`pushPull_sigma_iso`) — the single new-infra leaf;
20	3. identifies the sections of each leg over an open `V` with `Γ(U_σ ∩ V, F)`
21	   (`pushPull_leg_sections`);
22	4. assembles the degreewise section isomorphism `Γ(V, pushPullObj F Y_p) ≅ ∏_σ Γ(U_σ ∩ V, F)`
23	   (`pushPull_eval_prod_iso`);
24	5. promotes these degreewise isos to a complex isomorphism
25	   `D ≅ (sectionCechComplexV 𝒰 F V).augment ε hε`
26	   (`cechSection_complex_iso`); and
27	6. produces the contracting homotopy on the augmented concrete complex whenever
28	   `V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i_fix` (`cechSection_contractible`).
29	
30	The result is consumed by `CechAugmentedResolution.lean` to close the residual `hSec`.
31

48	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — abstract wide-fibre-power ↔ slice-product
49	
50	These `CategoryTheory`-namespace declarations are the abstract categorical core of the Stub-1
51	geometric backbone decomposition (`cechBackbone_left_sigma`).  They are stated for an arbitrary
52	category (and, downstream, an arbitrary `FinitaryPreExtensive` category) and instantiated at
53	`Scheme` only at the assembly site, so they are reusable and Mathlib-aligned.
54	-/
55	
56	namespace CategoryTheory
57	
58	/-- The wide pullback over `S` of a family `(g k : Z k ⟶ S)`, viewed in `Over S`, is a limit fan of
59	the legs `Over.mk (g k)`: i.e. the wide fibre power over `S` is the product of the legs in the slice
60	`Over S`.  Project-local: Mathlib has `WidePullbackCone.isLimitOfFan` (wide pullback over a terminal
61	base = product of legs) but not this direct slice-product identification of the fibre power. -/
62	noncomputable def widePullback_overX_isLimit {C : Type*} [Category C] {S : C}
63	    {ι : Type*} {Z : ι → C} (g : (k : ι) → Z k ⟶ S) [HasWidePullback S Z g] :
64	    IsLimit (Fan.mk (C := Over S) (f := fun k => Over.mk (g k))
65	      (Over.mk (WidePullback.base g))
66	      (fun k => Over.homMk (WidePullback.π g k) (WidePullback.π_arrow g k))) :=
67	  mkFanLimit _
68	    (fun s => Over.homMk
69	      (WidePullback.lift s.pt.hom (fun k => (s.proj k).left) (fun k => Over.w (s.proj k)))
70	      (WidePullback.lift_base _ _ _ _))
71	    (fun s k => by
72	      apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
73	      change (WidePullback.lift _ _ _ ≫ WidePullback.π g k) = (s.proj k).left
74	      exact WidePullback.lift_π _ _ _ _ _)
75	    (fun s m hm => by
76	      apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
77	      change m.left = WidePullback.lift _ _ _
78	      apply WidePullback.hom_ext
79	      · intro k
80	        rw [WidePullback.lift_π]
81	        exact congrArg CommaMorphism.left (hm k)
82	      · rw [WidePullback.lift_base]
83	        exact Over.w m)
84	
85	/-- The wide fibre power over `S` is the iterated product in the slice: in `Over S` the object
86	`Over.mk (WidePullback.base g)` carrying the wide pullback over `S` is the product
87	`∏ᶜ fun k => Over.mk (g k)` of the legs.  Project-local foundational step of the Stub-1 backbone
88	decomposition (blueprint `lem:widePullback_overX_eq_prod`). -/
89	noncomputable def widePullback_overX_eq_prod {C : Type*} [Category C] {S : C}
90	    {ι : Type*} {Z : ι → C} (g : (k : ι) → Z k ⟶ S) [HasWidePullback S Z g]
91	    [HasProduct (fun k => Over.mk (g k))] :
92	    Over.mk (WidePullback.base g) ≅ ∏ᶜ fun k => Over.mk (g k) :=
93	  (widePullback_overX_isLimit g).conePointUniqueUpToIso (productIsProduct _)
94	
95	/-- The cofan exhibiting `Over.mk (Sigma.desc f)` as the coproduct of the legs `Over.mk (f i)` in
96	`Over S`.  Abstract version of `AlgebraicGeometry.coverArrowOverCofan`. -/
97	noncomputable def overSigmaDescCofan {C : Type*} [Category C] {S : C} {ι : Type*}
98	    {Z : ι → C} (f : (i : ι) → Z i ⟶ S) [HasCoproduct Z] :
99	    Cofan (fun i => Over.mk (f i)) :=
100	  Cofan.mk (Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc f))
101	    (fun i => Over.homMk (Limits.Sigma.ι Z i) (by simp [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]))
102	
103	/-- `overSigmaDescCofan` is a colimit: in `Over S` the object `Over.mk (Sigma.desc f)` is the
104	coproduct of the legs `Over.mk (f i)`.  Abstract version of
105	`AlgebraicGeometry.coverArrowOverIsColimit`. -/
106	noncomputable def overSigmaDescIsColimit {C : Type*} [Category C] {S : C} {ι : Type*}
107	    {Z : ι → C} (f : (i : ι) → Z i ⟶ S) [HasCoproduct Z] :
108	    IsColimit (overSigmaDescCofan f) := by
109	  haveI : HasCoproduct (fun i => (Over.mk (f i)).left) := (inferInstanceAs (HasCoproduct Z))
110	  refine mkCofanColimit _
111	    (fun t => Over.homMk (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (t.inj i).left)) ?_)
112	    (fun t j => ?_) (fun t m hm => ?_)
113	  · change Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (t.inj i).left) ≫ t.pt.hom = Limits.Sigma.desc f
114	    refine Limits.Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun i => ?_)
115	    rw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Over.w]
116	    exact (Limits.Sigma.ι_desc f i).symm
117	  · apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
118	    simp [overSigmaDescCofan, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
119	  · apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
120	    refine Limits.Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun i => ?_)
121	    have h := congrArg CommaMorphism.left (hm i)
122	    simp only [overSigmaDescCofan, Cofan.inj, Cofan.mk,
123	      Discrete.natTrans_app, Over.homMk_left, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc] at h ⊢
124	    exact h
125	
126	/-- In `Over S`, the object `Over.mk (Sigma.desc f)` carrying the descent map is the coproduct of
127	the legs `Over.mk (f i)`.  Abstract version of `AlgebraicGeometry.coverArrowOverSigmaIso`; used to
128	rewrite the inner `∐ᵢ Z i` of the Stub-1 fibre power inside the slice. -/
129	noncomputable def overSigmaDescIso {C : Type*} [Category C] {S : C} {ι : Type*}
130	    {Z : ι → C} (f : (i : ι) → Z i ⟶ S) [HasCoproduct Z]
131	    [HasCoproduct (fun i => Over.mk (f i))] :
132	    (∐ fun i => Over.mk (f i)) ≅ Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc f) :=
133	  (coproductIsCoproduct _).coconePointUniqueUpToIso (overSigmaDescIsColimit f)
134	
135	namespace FinitaryPreExtensive
136	
137	/-- Splitting a finite product off its head: `∏ᶜ X ≅ X 0 ⨯ ∏ᶜ (fun i => X i.succ)` for
138	`X : Fin (n+1) → C`.  Built directly as a limit fan (`mkFanLimit`) — Mathlib has no `Fin`-succ
139	categorical product split.  This is the recursion that lets the wide fibre power
140	`∏ᶜ (Fin(p+2) copies)` be peeled into `head ×_S ∏ᶜ (Fin(p+1) copies)` in the slice during the
141	induction of `widePullback_coproduct_iso`.  Project-local. -/
142	noncomputable def prodFinSuccIso {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasFiniteProducts C] {n : ℕ}
143	    (X : Fin (n + 1) → C) :
144	    (∏ᶜ X) ≅ X 0 ⨯ (∏ᶜ fun i : Fin n => X i.succ) := by
145	  refine (productIsProduct X).conePointUniqueUpToIso (mkFanLimit
146	    (Fan.mk (X 0 ⨯ (∏ᶜ fun i : Fin n => X i.succ))
147	      (fun j => Fin.cases prod.fst (fun i => prod.snd ≫ Pi.π (fun i : Fin n => X i.succ) i) j))
148	    (fun s => prod.lift (s.proj 0) (Pi.lift (fun i : Fin n => s.proj i.succ)))
149	    (fun s j => ?_) (fun s m hm => ?_))
150	  · refine Fin.cases ?_ ?_ j
151	    · change prod.lift (s.proj 0) _ ≫ prod.fst = s.proj 0
152	      rw [prod.lift_fst]
153	    · intro i
154	      change prod.lift _ _ ≫ (prod.snd ≫ Pi.π (fun i : Fin n => X i.succ) i) = s.proj i.succ
155	      rw [← Category.assoc, prod.lift_snd, Pi.lift_π]
156	  · apply prod.hom_ext
157	    · rw [prod.lift_fst]
158	      have := hm 0; change m ≫ prod.fst = s.proj 0 at this; exact this
159	    · rw [prod.lift_snd]
160	      apply Pi.hom_ext; intro i
161	      rw [Pi.lift_π]
162	      have := hm i.succ
163	      change m ≫ (prod.snd ≫ Pi.π (fun i : Fin n => X i.succ) i) = s.proj i.succ at this
164	      rw [← Category.assoc] at this; exact this
165	
166	/-- One-sided distributivity of the fibre product over a coproduct in `C`:
167	`∐ᵢ (A ×_S Y i) ≅ A ×_S (∐ᵢ Y i)`.  Derived from `isIso_sigmaDesc_fst` (universality of finite
168	coproducts in a finitary pre-extensive category) by pulling the coproduct injections back along the
169	second projection of `A ×_S ∐Y` (pasting via `pullbackLeftPullbackSndIso`).  Blueprint
170	`lem:prod_coproduct_distrib` (the `C`-level fibre-product form of the one-sided distributivity used
171	in the induction of `widePullback_coproduct_iso`). -/
172	noncomputable def prod_coproduct_distrib {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasPullbacks C]
173	    [FinitaryPreExtensive C] {ι : Type} [Finite ι] {S : C} (A : C) (a : A ⟶ S)
174	    {Y : ι → C} (g : (i : ι) → Y i ⟶ S) :
175	    (∐ fun i => pullback a (g i)) ≅ pullback a (Limits.Sigma.desc g) := by
176	  have hπ : IsIso (Limits.Sigma.desc (Limits.Sigma.ι Y)) := by
177	    rw [show Limits.Sigma.desc (Limits.Sigma.ι Y) = 𝟙 _ from
178	      Limits.Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun i => by rw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, Category.comp_id])]
179	    infer_instance
180	  have key := FinitaryPreExtensive.isIso_sigmaDesc_fst (Limits.Sigma.ι Y)
181	    (pullback.snd a (Limits.Sigma.desc g)) hπ
182	  let e : (i : ι) → pullback (pullback.snd a (Limits.Sigma.desc g)) (Limits.Sigma.ι Y i)
183	      ≅ pullback a (g i) := fun i =>
184	    pullbackLeftPullbackSndIso a (Limits.Sigma.desc g) (Limits.Sigma.ι Y i) ≪≫
185	      pullback.congrHom rfl (by rw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc])
186	  exact (Limits.Sigma.mapIso e).symm ≪≫ asIso (Limits.Sigma.desc
187	    (fun i => pullback.fst (pullback.snd a (Limits.Sigma.desc g)) (Limits.Sigma.ι Y i)))
188	
189	/-- Nested-coproduct flatten + `Fin.cons` reindex: `∐ᵢ ∐_τ F(cons i τ) ≅ ∐_σ F σ` over the
190	`(p+2)`-fold multi-indices.  Pure reindexing (`sigmaSigmaIso` collapses the nested coproduct; the
191	`Fin.consEquiv` reindexes the resulting pairs `(i, τ) ↦ Fin.cons i τ`).  Blueprint
192	`lem:coproduct_fibrePower_reindex`.  The identification of the component `F (Fin.cons i τ)` with the
193	`(p+2)`-fold fibre power lives in the inductive step that consumes this lemma. -/
194	noncomputable def coproduct_fibrePower_reindex {C : Type*} [Category C] {ι : Type} [Finite ι]
195	    [HasFiniteCoproducts C] (p : ℕ) (F : (Fin (p + 2) → ι) → C) :
196	    (∐ fun i : ι => ∐ fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → ι => F (Fin.cons i τ))
197	      ≅ ∐ fun σ : Fin (p + 2) → ι => F σ :=
198	  sigmaSigmaIso (fun _ : ι => (Fin (p + 1) → ι)) (fun i τ => F (Fin.cons i τ)) ≪≫
199	  Sigma.whiskerEquiv
200	    ((Equiv.sigmaEquivProd ι (Fin (p + 1) → ι)).trans (Fin.consEquiv (fun _ => ι)))
201	    (fun _ => Iso.refl _)
202	
203	/-- Base case of the wide-fibre-power decomposition (`p = 0`): the `1`-fold wide fibre power of the
204	descent map `∐ᵢ Z i ⟶ S` over `S` is, in `Over S`, the coproduct over `σ : Fin 1 → ι` of the
205	`1`-fold fibre powers (here written as products in the slice).  Pure reindexing — no extensivity
206	needed — chaining `widePullback_overX_eq_prod`, `productUniqueIso` (over `Fin 1`), `overSigmaDescIso`
207	and the coproduct reindex along `(Fin 1 → ι) ≃ ι`.  Blueprint `lem:coproduct_distrib_fibrePower_zero`.
208	
209	Note: the σ-component is the slice product `∏ᶜ fun k => Over.mk (f (σ k))`, which is identified with
210	the wide fibre power `Over.mk (WidePullback.base (fun k => f (σ k)))` via `widePullback_overX_eq_prod`
211	at the assembly site.  This is the project's chosen (slice-product) normal form for the components,
212	which minimizes the `HasWidePullback` instance bookkeeping in the induction. -/
213	noncomputable def widePullback_coproduct_iso_zero {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasPullbacks C]
214	    {S : C} {ι : Type*} [Finite ι] {Z : ι → C} (f : (i : ι) → Z i ⟶ S)
215	    [HasFiniteWidePullbacks C] [HasFiniteCoproducts C]
216	    [HasFiniteProducts (Over S)] [HasFiniteCoproducts (Over S)] :
217	    Over.mk (WidePullback.base (fun _ : Fin 1 => Limits.Sigma.desc f))
218	      ≅ ∐ (fun σ : Fin 1 → ι => ∏ᶜ (fun k : Fin 1 => Over.mk (f (σ k)))) :=
219	  widePullback_overX_eq_prod (fun _ : Fin 1 => Limits.Sigma.desc f) ≪≫
220	  productUniqueIso (fun _ : Fin 1 => Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc f)) ≪≫
221	  (overSigmaDescIso f).symm ≪≫
222	  Sigma.whiskerEquiv (Equiv.funUnique (Fin 1) ι).symm
223	    (fun i => productUniqueIso
224	      (fun k : Fin 1 => Over.mk (f (((Equiv.funUnique (Fin 1) ι).symm i) k))))
225	
226	
227	/-- One-sided distributivity in `Over S`'s underlying category, coproduct in the FIRST pullback
228	argument: `∐ᵢ pullback (gᵢ) b ≅ pullback (Sigma.desc g) b`.  Derived from the project's
229	`prod_coproduct_distrib` (coproduct-second form) by conjugating with `pullbackSymmetry`.
230	Project-local: consumed by `overProd_coproduct_distrib`'s structure-map compatibility. -/
231	noncomputable def coprodFirst_distrib {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasPullbacks C] [FinitaryPreExtensive C] {ι : Type} [Finite ι] {S : C}
232	    (B : C) (b : B ⟶ S) {Y : ι → C} (g : (i : ι) → Y i ⟶ S) :
233	    (∐ fun i => pullback (g i) b) ≅ pullback (Limits.Sigma.desc g) b :=
234	  asIso (Limits.Sigma.map (fun i => (pullbackSymmetry (g i) b).hom)) ≪≫
235	    prod_coproduct_distrib B b g ≪≫ pullbackSymmetry b (Limits.Sigma.desc g)
236	
237	
238	/-- `prod_coproduct_distrib` is compatible with the first projection to the base of `a`:
239	its hom followed by `pullback.fst` is the descent of the per-summand `pullback.fst`. Project-local
240	compatibility lemma used to verify the structure-map condition of `overProd_coproduct_distrib`. -/
241	lemma pcd_hom_fst {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasPullbacks C] [FinitaryPreExtensive C] {ι : Type} [Finite ι] {S : C} (A : C) (a : A ⟶ S) {Y : ι → C} (g : (i : ι) → Y i ⟶ S) :
242	    (prod_coproduct_distrib A a g).hom ≫ pullback.fst a (Limits.Sigma.desc g)
243	      = Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => pullback.fst a (g i)) := by
244	  refine Limits.Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun i => ?_)
245	  rw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
246	  have hstep : (prod_coproduct_distrib A a g).hom
247	      = (Limits.Sigma.map (fun i => (pullbackLeftPullbackSndIso a (Limits.Sigma.desc g) (Sigma.ι Y i) ≪≫
248	            pullback.congrHom rfl (by rw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc])).inv)) ≫
249	          Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => pullback.fst (pullback.snd a (Limits.Sigma.desc g)) (Sigma.ι Y i)) := by
250	    simp only [prod_coproduct_distrib, Iso.trans_hom, Iso.symm_hom, asIso_hom]
251	    congr 1
252	  rw [hstep]
253	  simp only [Category.assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_map_assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc]
254	  simp
255	  simp only [pullback.map]
256	  rw [pullback.lift_fst]
257	  simp
258	
259	/-- `prod_coproduct_distrib` compatibility with the second projection (the coproduct side):
260	its hom followed by `pullback.snd` descends to the per-summand `pullback.snd ≫ Sigma.ι`.
261	Project-local compatibility lemma for `overProd_coproduct_distrib`. -/
262	lemma pcd_hom_snd {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasPullbacks C] [FinitaryPreExtensive C] {ι : Type} [Finite ι] {S : C} (A : C) (a : A ⟶ S) {Y : ι → C} (g : (i : ι) → Y i ⟶ S) :
263	    (prod_coproduct_distrib A a g).hom ≫ pullback.snd a (Limits.Sigma.desc g)
264	      = Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => pullback.snd a (g i) ≫ Sigma.ι Y i) := by
265	  refine Limits.Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun i => ?_)
266	  rw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
267	  have hstep : (prod_coproduct_distrib A a g).hom
268	      = (Limits.Sigma.map (fun i => (pullbackLeftPullbackSndIso a (Limits.Sigma.desc g) (Sigma.ι Y i) ≪≫
269	            pullback.congrHom rfl (by rw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc])).inv)) ≫
270	          Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => pullback.fst (pullback.snd a (Limits.Sigma.desc g)) (Sigma.ι Y i)) := by
271	    simp only [prod_coproduct_distrib, Iso.trans_hom, Iso.symm_hom, asIso_hom]
272	    congr 1
273	  rw [hstep]
274	  simp only [Category.assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_map_assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc]
275	  rw [pullback.condition]
276	  simp
277	  simp only [pullback.map]
278	  rw [pullback.lift_snd_assoc]
279	  simp
280	
281	/-- `coprodFirst_distrib` compatibility with `pullback.fst` (the coproduct side): descends to the
282	per-summand `pullback.fst ≫ Sigma.ι`.  Project-local, used in `overProd_coproduct_distrib`. -/
283	lemma cf_hom_fst {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasPullbacks C] [FinitaryPreExtensive C] {ι : Type} [Finite ι] {S : C} (B : C) (b : B ⟶ S) {Y : ι → C} (g : (i : ι) → Y i ⟶ S) :
284	    (coprodFirst_distrib B b g).hom ≫ pullback.fst (Limits.Sigma.desc g) b
285	      = Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => pullback.fst (g i) b ≫ Sigma.ι Y i) := by
286	  rw [coprodFirst_distrib]
287	  simp only [Iso.trans_hom, asIso_hom, Category.assoc]
288	  rw [pullbackSymmetry_hom_comp_fst, pcd_hom_snd]
289	  refine Limits.Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun j => ?_)
290	  rw [← Category.assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_map, Category.assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc,
291	    ← Category.assoc, pullbackSymmetry_hom_comp_snd, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
292	
293	private lemma overSigma_hom_eq {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasPullbacks C] {S : C} {ι : Type} [Finite ι] [HasFiniteCoproducts C]
294	    (A : ι → Over S) :
295	    (∐ A).hom = (PreservesCoproduct.iso (Over.forget S) A).hom ≫
296	      Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i).hom) := by
297	  haveI : HasColimit (Discrete.functor A ⋙ Over.forget S) :=
298	    hasColimit_of_iso (F := Discrete.functor (fun i => (A i).left))
299	      (Discrete.natIso (fun i => Iso.refl _))
300	  refine (PreservesCoproduct.iso (Over.forget S) A).inv_comp_eq.mp ?_
301	  rw [PreservesCoproduct.inv_hom]
302	  refine Limits.Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun i => ?_)
303	  rw [ι_comp_sigmaComparison_assoc]
304	  show (Sigma.ι A i).left ≫ (∐ A).hom = _
305	  rw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
306	  exact Over.w (Sigma.ι A i)
307	
308	/-- One-sided distributivity of the binary product over a finite coproduct in the slice category
309	`Over S` of a finitary pre-extensive category: `(∐ᵢ Aᵢ) ⨯ B ≅ ∐ᵢ (Aᵢ ⨯ B)`.  Built via
310	`Over.isoMk` from the `C`-level `prod_coproduct_distrib`, threading `Over.prodLeftIsoPullback`
311	and the coproduct-preservation of `Over.forget`.  This is the slice-product distributivity the
312	inductive step of `widePullback_coproduct_iso` consumes (blueprint `lem:overProd_coproduct_distrib`). -/
313	noncomputable def overProd_coproduct_distrib {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasPullbacks C] [FinitaryPreExtensive C] {ι : Type} [Finite ι] [HasFiniteCoproducts C]
314	    {S : C} [HasBinaryProducts (Over S)] (A : ι → Over S) (B : Over S) :
315	    (∐ A) ⨯ B ≅ ∐ fun i => A i ⨯ B := by
316	  set pA := PreservesCoproduct.iso (Over.forget S) A with hpA
317	  set pAB := PreservesCoproduct.iso (Over.forget S) (fun i => A i ⨯ B) with hpAB
318	  have hA : (∐ A).hom = pA.hom ≫ Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i).hom) := overSigma_hom_eq A
319	  have hAB : (∐ fun i => A i ⨯ B).hom
320	      = pAB.hom ≫ Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i ⨯ B).hom) := overSigma_hom_eq (fun i => A i ⨯ B)
321	  clear_value pA pAB
322	  have hAB' : pAB.inv ≫ (∐ fun i => A i ⨯ B).hom
323	      = Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i ⨯ B).hom) := by
324	    rw [hAB]; simp
325	  have hcond : (∐ A).hom ≫ 𝟙 S = pA.hom ≫ Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i).hom) := by
326	    rw [Category.comp_id]; exact hA
327	  refine Over.isoMk (Over.prodLeftIsoPullback (∐ A) B ≪≫
328	    asIso (pullback.map (∐ A).hom B.hom (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i).hom)) B.hom
329	      pA.hom (𝟙 B.left) (𝟙 S) hcond (by simp)) ≪≫
330	    (coprodFirst_distrib B.left B.hom (fun i => (A i).hom)).symm ≪≫
331	    asIso (Limits.Sigma.map (fun i => (Over.prodLeftIsoPullback (A i) B).inv)) ≪≫
332	    pAB.symm) ?_
333	  show _ ≫ (∐ fun i => A i ⨯ B).hom = ((∐ A) ⨯ B).hom
334	  have hR : ((∐ A) ⨯ B).hom
335	      = (Over.prodLeftIsoPullback (∐ A) B).hom ≫ pullback.fst (∐ A).hom B.hom ≫ (∐ A).hom := by
336	    rw [← Over.w (prod.fst (X := ∐ A) (Y := B)), ← Over.prodLeftIsoPullback_hom_fst, Category.assoc]
337	  have e3eq : (coprodFirst_distrib B.left B.hom (fun i => (A i).hom)).inv ≫
338	      Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => pullback.fst (A i).hom B.hom ≫ (A i).hom)
339	      = pullback.fst (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i).hom)) B.hom ≫
340	          Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i).hom) := by
341	    rw [Iso.inv_comp_eq, ← Category.assoc, cf_hom_fst]
342	    refine Limits.Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun i => ?_)
343	    simp only [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Category.assoc]
344	  have e4eq : Limits.Sigma.map (fun i => (Over.prodLeftIsoPullback (A i) B).inv) ≫
345	        Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => (A i ⨯ B).hom)
346	        = Limits.Sigma.desc (fun i => pullback.fst (A i).hom B.hom ≫ (A i).hom) := by
347	    refine Limits.Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun i => ?_)
348	    rw [← Category.assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_map, Category.assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc,
349	      Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, ← Over.w (prod.fst (X := A i) (Y := B)),
350	      ← Over.prodLeftIsoPullback_inv_fst_assoc]
351	  rw [hR]
352	  simp only [Iso.trans_hom, asIso_hom, Category.assoc]
353	  simp only [Iso.symm_hom]
354	  erw [hAB']
355	  rw [e4eq, e3eq]
356	  congr 1
357	  rw [← Category.assoc]
358	  simp only [pullback.map]
359	  rw [pullback.lift_fst, Category.assoc]
360	  exact congrArg _ hA.symm
361	
362	
363	/-- Right-handed one-sided distributivity in `Over S`: `A ⨯ (∐ᵢ Yᵢ) ≅ ∐ᵢ (A ⨯ Yᵢ)`. -/
364	noncomputable def overProd_coproduct_distrib_right {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasPullbacks C]
365	    [FinitaryPreExtensive C] {ι : Type} [Finite ι] [HasFiniteCoproducts C]
366	    {S : C} [HasBinaryProducts (Over S)] (A : Over S) (Y : ι → Over S) :
367	    A ⨯ (∐ Y) ≅ ∐ fun i => A ⨯ Y i :=
368	  Limits.prod.braiding A (∐ Y) ≪≫ overProd_coproduct_distrib Y A ≪≫
369	    Limits.Sigma.mapIso (fun i => Limits.prod.braiding (Y i) A)
370	
371	-- The inductive step chains six iso layers (`widePullback_overX_eq_prod`, `prodFinSuccIso`,
372	-- two distributivity isos, the reindex), whose combined `whnf` over the nested fibre powers
373	-- exceeds the default heartbeat budget.
374	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
375	/-- Coproduct distributes over the `(p+1)`-fold wide fibre power of the cover map `∐ᵢ Zᵢ ⟶ S`,
376	in slice-product normal form (blueprint `lem:coproduct_distrib_fibrePower`). -/
377	noncomputable def widePullback_coproduct_iso {C : Type*} [Category C] [HasPullbacks C]
378	    [FinitaryPreExtensive C] {S : C} {ι : Type} [Finite ι] {Z : ι → C}
379	    (f : (i : ι) → Z i ⟶ S) [HasFiniteWidePullbacks C] [HasFiniteCoproducts C]
380	    [HasFiniteProducts (Over S)] [HasFiniteCoproducts (Over S)] :
381	    (p : ℕ) → (Over.mk (WidePullback.base (fun _ : Fin (p + 1) => Limits.Sigma.desc f))
382	      ≅ ∐ (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι => ∏ᶜ (fun k : Fin (p + 1) => Over.mk (f (σ k)))))
383	  | 0 => widePullback_coproduct_iso_zero f
384	  | (p+1) => by
385	      refine widePullback_overX_eq_prod (fun _ : Fin (p + 2) => Limits.Sigma.desc f) ≪≫
386	        prodFinSuccIso (fun _ : Fin (p + 2) => Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc f)) ≪≫ ?_
387	      refine Limits.prod.mapIso (overSigmaDescIso f).symm
388	        ((widePullback_overX_eq_prod (fun _ : Fin (p + 1) => Limits.Sigma.desc f)).symm ≪≫
389	          widePullback_coproduct_iso f p) ≪≫ ?_
390	      refine overProd_coproduct_distrib (fun i => Over.mk (f i))
391	        (∐ fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → ι => ∏ᶜ fun k => Over.mk (f (τ k))) ≪≫ ?_
392	      refine Limits.Sigma.mapIso (fun i => overProd_coproduct_distrib_right (Over.mk (f i))
393	        (fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → ι => ∏ᶜ fun k => Over.mk (f (τ k)))) ≪≫ ?_
394	      have e7 := Limits.Sigma.mapIso (fun i => Limits.Sigma.mapIso (fun τ : Fin (p + 1) → ι =>
395	        (prodFinSuccIso (fun k : Fin (p + 2) => Over.mk (f (Fin.cons i τ k)))).symm))
396	      exact e7 ≪≫ coproduct_fibrePower_reindex p (fun σ => ∏ᶜ fun k => Over.mk (f (σ k)))
397	
398	end FinitaryPreExtensive
399	
400	/-- A coproduct indexed by `Option α` splits off its `none` summand:
401	`∐ Z ≅ Z none ⨿ (∐ a, Z (some a))`.  Project-local: the coproduct reassociation that drives the
402	`Option`-step of the finite-index induction in `pushPull_coprod_prod` (Mathlib has `sigmaSigmaIso`
403	for nested coproducts but no `Option`-split). -/
404	noncomputable def sigmaOptionIso {C : Type*} [Category C] {α : Type*} (Z : Option α → C)
405	    [HasCoproduct Z] [HasCoproduct (fun a : α => Z (some a))]
406	    [HasBinaryCoproduct (Z none) (∐ fun a : α => Z (some a))] :
407	    (∐ Z) ≅ Z none ⨿ (∐ fun a : α => Z (some a)) where
408	  hom := Limits.Sigma.desc
409	    (fun o => Option.rec Limits.coprod.inl
410	      (fun a => Limits.Sigma.ι (fun a : α => Z (some a)) a ≫ Limits.coprod.inr) o)
411	  inv := Limits.coprod.desc (Limits.Sigma.ι Z none)
412	    (Limits.Sigma.desc (fun a => Limits.Sigma.ι Z (some a)))
413	  hom_inv_id := by
414	    apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext
415	    rintro (_ | a)
416	    · simp [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Limits.coprod.inl_desc]
417	    · simp [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Limits.coprod.inr_desc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
418	  inv_hom_id := by
419	    apply Limits.coprod.hom_ext
420	    · simp [Limits.coprod.inl_desc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
421	    · rw [Category.comp_id]
422	      apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext
423	      intro a
424	      simp [Limits.coprod.inr_desc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
425	
426	end CategoryTheory

428	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
429	
430	open Scheme.Modules
431	
432	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
433	
434	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Stub 1 geometric backbone
435	
436	The degree-`p` Čech-nerve backbone `(coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj (op [p])` is the `(p+1)`-fold
437	fibre power of the cover map `q = Sigma.desc 𝒰.f` over `X`.  Identifying it with the coproduct
438	`∐_σ (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)` needs two geometric ingredients that Mathlib provides only in binary
439	form:
440	
441	* `widePullback_openImm_inter` — the wide pullback over `X` of a *finite* family of open
442	  immersions is the intersection of their open ranges (built here from `IsOpenImmersion.lift`
443	  and the wide-pullback universal property);
444	* the wide/iterated coproduct-distributes-over-fibre-power step (extensivity), still open.
445	-/
446	
447	open TopologicalSpace in
448	/-- Pointwise membership in a *finite* intersection of opens.  Project-local because Mathlib's
449	`Opens` infimum is interior-based, so the pointwise `mem_iInf` characterization only holds for
450	finite families. -/
451	private lemma mem_iInf_opens_of_finite {Y : Type*} [TopologicalSpace Y] {κ : Type}
452	    [Finite κ] (f : κ → Opens Y) (x : Y) :
453	    x ∈ (⨅ i, f i : Opens Y) ↔ ∀ i, x ∈ f i := by
454	  rw [← SetLike.mem_coe,
455	    show ((⨅ i, f i : Opens Y) : Set Y) = ⋂ i, (f i : Set Y) from ?_]
456	  · simp
457	  · apply le_antisymm
458	    · exact Set.subset_iInter fun i => SetLike.coe_subset_coe.mpr (iInf_le f i)
459	    · have hopen : IsOpen (⋂ i, (f i : Set Y)) := isOpen_iInter_of_finite fun i => (f i).2
460	      have hW : (⟨⋂ i, (f i : Set Y), hopen⟩ : Opens Y) ≤ ⨅ i, f i :=
461	        le_iInf fun i => by intro y hy; exact Set.mem_iInter.mp hy i
462	      exact hW
463	
464	/-- The wide pullback over `X` of a finite family of open immersions `g k : Z k ⟶ X` is the
465	intersection open `⨅ k, (g k).opensRange`.  Project-local: Mathlib has the binary
466	`isPullback_opens_inf` but not this wide/iterated form, needed to identify each summand of the
467	distributed Čech-nerve backbone with a `coverInterOpen`.  (With `g k := 𝒰.f (σ k)` the target
468	open is exactly `coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ`.) -/
469	noncomputable def widePullback_openImm_inter {κ : Type} [Finite κ]
470	    {Z : κ → Scheme.{u}} (g : (k : κ) → Z k ⟶ X) [∀ k, IsOpenImmersion (g k)] :
471	    widePullback X Z g ≅ (⨅ k, (g k).opensRange).toScheme where
472	  hom := by
473	    refine IsOpenImmersion.lift (⨅ k, (g k).opensRange).ι (WidePullback.base g) ?_
474	    rw [Scheme.Opens.range_ι]
475	    rintro x ⟨w, rfl⟩
476	    rw [SetLike.mem_coe, mem_iInf_opens_of_finite]
477	    intro k
478	    have hcomp : WidePullback.base g w = (g k) (WidePullback.π g k w) := by
479	      rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, WidePullback.π_arrow]
480	    rw [hcomp, ← SetLike.mem_coe, Scheme.Hom.coe_opensRange]
481	    exact Set.mem_range_self _
482	  inv := WidePullback.lift (⨅ k, (g k).opensRange).ι
483	    (fun k => IsOpenImmersion.lift (g k) (⨅ k, (g k).opensRange).ι (by
484	      rw [Scheme.Opens.range_ι, ← Scheme.Hom.coe_opensRange]
485	      exact SetLike.coe_subset_coe.mpr (iInf_le _ k)))
486	    (fun k => IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac _ _ _)
487	  hom_inv_id := by
488	    apply WidePullback.hom_ext g
489	    · intro j
490	      rw [Category.assoc, Category.id_comp, WidePullback.lift_π,
491	        ← cancel_mono (g j), Category.assoc, IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac,
492	        IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac, WidePullback.π_arrow]
493	    · rw [Category.assoc, Category.id_comp, WidePullback.lift_base, IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac]
494	  inv_hom_id := by
495	    rw [← cancel_mono (⨅ k, (g k).opensRange).ι, Category.assoc, Category.id_comp,
496	      IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac, WidePullback.lift_base]
497	
498	/-- The cofan exhibiting `Over.mk (Sigma.desc 𝒰.f)` as the coproduct of the member arrows
499	`Over.mk (𝒰.f i)` in `Over X`.  Project-local: the coproduct cocone of the cover arrow. -/
500	noncomputable def coverArrowOverCofan (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) :
501	    Cofan (fun i : 𝒰.I₀ => Over.mk (𝒰.f i)) :=
502	  Cofan.mk (Over.mk (Sigma.desc 𝒰.f))
503	    (fun i => Over.homMk (Sigma.ι 𝒰.X i) (by simp [Sigma.ι_desc]))
504	
505	/-- `coverArrowOverCofan` is a colimit: in `Over X` the cover arrow `Over.mk (Sigma.desc 𝒰.f)`
506	is the coproduct of the member arrows.  Proved directly from the coproduct universal property in
507	`Scheme` (each leg is `Over.homMk (Sigma.ι …)`).  Project-local. -/
508	noncomputable def coverArrowOverIsColimit (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) :
509	    IsColimit (coverArrowOverCofan 𝒰) := by
510	  refine mkCofanColimit _
511	    (fun t => Over.homMk (Sigma.desc (fun i => (t.inj i).left)) ?_)
512	    (fun t j => ?_) (fun t m hm => ?_)
513	  · change Sigma.desc (fun i => (t.inj i).left) ≫ t.pt.hom = Sigma.desc 𝒰.f
514	    refine Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun i => ?_)
515	    rw [Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Over.w]
516	    exact (Sigma.ι_desc 𝒰.f i).symm
517	  · apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
518	    simp [coverArrowOverCofan, Sigma.ι_desc]
519	  · apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
520	    refine Sigma.hom_ext _ _ (fun i => ?_)
521	    have h := congrArg CommaMorphism.left (hm i)
522	    simp only [coverArrowOverCofan, Cofan.inj, Cofan.mk,
523	      Discrete.natTrans_app, Over.homMk_left, Sigma.ι_desc] at h ⊢
524	    exact h
525	
526	/-- In `Over X`, the cover arrow `Over.mk (Sigma.desc 𝒰.f)` is the coproduct of the member
527	arrows `Over.mk (𝒰.f i)`.  Project-local component of the Stub-1 distributivity step: the inner
528	`∐ᵢ Uᵢ` of the fibre power, transported into `Over X`. -/
529	noncomputable def coverArrowOverSigmaIso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) :
530	    (∐ fun i : 𝒰.I₀ => Over.mk (𝒰.f i)) ≅ Over.mk (Sigma.desc 𝒰.f) :=
531	  (coproductIsCoproduct _).coconePointUniqueUpToIso (coverArrowOverIsColimit 𝒰)
532	
533	/-- Transport of the `m`-fold wide fibre power (constant family) of a single map `q : A ⟶ X` along
534	an isomorphism `w : B ≅ A` of the apex with `w.hom ≫ q = q'`: in `Over X` the backbones
535	`Over.mk (WidePullback.base (fun _ => q))` and `Over.mk (WidePullback.base (fun _ => q'))` agree.
536	Specialized to `Scheme` (the hom-universe must be pinned for the `rw`/`simp` on composites to fire).
537	Used in `cechBackbone_left_sigma` to transport the cover-arrow fibre power across the
538	universe-reduction reindexing `∐ 𝒰.X ≅ ∐ (𝒰.X ∘ e.symm)`. -/
539	noncomputable def widePullbackBaseCongr {A B : Scheme.{u}} (q : A ⟶ X) (q' : B ⟶ X)
540	    (w : B ≅ A) (hw : w.hom ≫ q = q') (m : ℕ) :
541	    Over.mk (WidePullback.base (fun _ : Fin m => q)) ≅
542	    Over.mk (WidePullback.base (fun _ : Fin m => q')) := by
543	  have hinv : w.inv ≫ q' = q := by rw [← hw, ← Category.assoc, w.inv_hom_id, Category.id_comp]
544	  refine Over.isoMk ?_ ?_
545	  · refine
546	      { hom := WidePullback.lift (WidePullback.base _)
547	          (fun k => WidePullback.π (fun _ : Fin m => q) k ≫ w.inv)

693	/-- The binary disjoint-cover decomposition comparison map of a module sheaf on a coproduct
694	scheme: the pair of restriction-to-component units. -/
695	private noncomputable def coprodDecompMap (M : (A ⨿ B).Modules) :
696	    M ⟶ (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (coprod.inl : A ⟶ A ⨿ B)).obj
697	          (M.restrict (coprod.inl : A ⟶ A ⨿ B)) ⨯
698	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (coprod.inr : B ⟶ A ⨿ B)).obj
699	          (M.restrict (coprod.inr : B ⟶ A ⨿ B)) :=
700	  prod.lift ((Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction (coprod.inl : A ⟶ A ⨿ B)).unit.app M)
701	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction (coprod.inr : B ⟶ A ⨿ B)).unit.app M)

703	/- **Status: L2 DONE.** The disjoint-cover leaf `isIso_coprodDecompMap`, the per-leg coherence

705	are all proved axiom-clean below; the finite-index generalization `pushPull_coprod_prod` and
706	Stub 2 `pushPull_sigma_iso` chain them.  All three `coprodToProd_isIso_*` induction steps
707	(empty / option / equiv) are now closed, so `pushPull_sigma_iso` and Stub 4
708	`pushPull_eval_prod_iso` are axiom-clean. -/
709	
710	/-- The binary disjoint-cover decomposition comparison map `coprodDecompMap M` is an isomorphism:
711	a module sheaf on `A ⨿ B` is the product of its restrictions-to-component pushforwards.  This is the
712	binary disjoint-union sheaf decomposition (`TopCat.Sheaf.isProductOfDisjoint` on the underlying
713	abelian sheaf), reflected back to `(A ⨿ B).Modules`.  Project-local leaf of the Stub-2 chain. -/
714	private theorem isIso_coprodDecompMap (M : (A ⨿ B).Modules) :
715	    IsIso (coprodDecompMap M) := by
716	  rw [Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app]
717	  intro U
718	  set ι₀ : A ⟶ A ⨿ B := coprod.inl with hι₀
719	  set ι₁ : B ⟶ A ⨿ B := coprod.inr with hι₁
720	  set W₀ : (A ⨿ B).Opens := ι₀ ''ᵁ ι₀ ⁻¹ᵁ U with hW₀
721	  set W₁ : (A ⨿ B).Opens := ι₁ ''ᵁ ι₁ ⁻¹ᵁ U with hW₁
722	  have h₂ : W₀ ⊓ W₁ = ⊥ := by
723	    rw [hW₀, hW₁]
724	    simp_rw [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf]
725	    rw [← inf_inf_distrib_right, (isCompl_opensRange_inl_inr A B).inf_eq_bot, bot_inf_eq]
726	  have h₁ : W₀ ⊔ W₁ = U := by
727	    rw [hW₀, hW₁]
728	    simp_rw [Scheme.Hom.image_preimage_eq_opensRange_inf]
729	    rw [← inf_sup_right, (isCompl_opensRange_inl_inr A B).sup_eq_top, top_inf_eq]
730	  -- the two restriction-unit legs at `U`, identified as presheaf restriction maps
731	  set u₀ := (Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction ι₀).unit.app M with hu₀
732	  set u₁ := (Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction ι₁).unit.app M with hu₁
733	  have hleg₀ : u₀.app U = M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (ι₀.image_preimage_le U)).op := by
734	    rw [hu₀, Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction_unit_app_app]
735	  have hleg₁ : u₁.app U = M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (ι₁.image_preimage_le U)).op := by
736	    rw [hu₁, Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction_unit_app_app]
737	  -- the disjoint-union limit on the underlying abelian sheaf of `M`, transported to `Γ(M, U)`
738	  have LimAb : IsLimit (BinaryFan.mk (u₀.app U) (u₁.app U)) := by
739	    have L := TopCat.Sheaf.isProductOfDisjoint
740	      (⟨M.presheaf, M.isSheaf⟩ : TopCat.Sheaf Ab _) W₀ W₁ h₂
741	    refine L.ofIsoLimit (Cone.ext (M.presheaf.mapIso (eqToIso (congrArg Opposite.op h₁))) ?_)
742	    rintro ⟨(_ | _)⟩
743	    · show M.presheaf.map (homOfLE le_sup_left).op
744	        = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg Opposite.op h₁)) ≫ u₀.app U
745	      rw [hleg₀, show (homOfLE (le_sup_left : W₀ ≤ W₀ ⊔ W₁)).op
746	            = eqToHom (congrArg Opposite.op h₁) ≫ (homOfLE (ι₀.image_preimage_le U)).op
747	          from Subsingleton.elim _ _, M.presheaf.map_comp]
748	      rfl
749	    · show M.presheaf.map (homOfLE le_sup_right).op
750	        = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (congrArg Opposite.op h₁)) ≫ u₁.app U
751	      rw [hleg₁, show (homOfLE (le_sup_right : W₁ ≤ W₀ ⊔ W₁)).op
752	            = eqToHom (congrArg Opposite.op h₁) ≫ (homOfLE (ι₁.image_preimage_le U)).op
753	          from Subsingleton.elim _ _, M.presheaf.map_comp]
754	      rfl
755	  -- reflect the limit up to `ModuleCat` through the evaluation functor
756	  set G := SheafOfModules.evaluation (R := (A ⨿ B).ringCatSheaf) (Opposite.op U) with hGdef
757	  have LimMod : IsLimit (BinaryFan.mk (G.map u₀) (G.map u₁)) := by
758	    refine isLimitOfReflectsOfMapIsLimit (forget₂ (ModuleCat _) AddCommGrpCat)
759	      (G.map u₀) (G.map u₁) ?_
760	    exact LimAb
761	  -- conclude isomorphy of the comparison map at `U`
762	  haveI key : IsIso (G.map (coprodDecompMap M)) :=
763	    isIso_map_prodLift_of_isLimit G LimMod
764	  have happ : (coprodDecompMap M).app U
765	      = (forget₂ (ModuleCat _) AddCommGrpCat).map (G.map (coprodDecompMap M)) := rfl
766	  rw [happ]
767	  exact Functor.map_isIso _ _

769	/-! ### L2 `q_*`-coherence chain: push–pull turns a coproduct into a product
770	
771	The binary disjoint-cover decomposition `isIso_coprodDecompMap` is upgraded to a statement about
772	the push–pull object `pushPullObj F (Over.mk q)` (`q = coprod.desc pA pB`).  The single substantive
773	node is the per-leg coherence `pushPull_binary_leg_coherence` (★): the contravariant push–pull map of
774	the over-inclusion `Over.homMk c : Over.mk pC ⟶ Over.mk q` is, up to the canonical leg iso

776	canonical comparison `prod.lift (pushPullMap F overInl) (pushPullMap F overInr)` be matched against

779	/-- The canonical leg iso identifying `q_*(c_*(M.restrict c))` (a factor of `q_*(P ⨯ Q)`, where
780	`M = q^* F` and `c` is a coproduct inclusion `coprod.inl`/`coprod.inr`) with the push–pull object
781	`pushPullObj F (Over.mk pC)` along the over-triangle `wC : c ≫ q = pC`.  Project-local component of
782	the L2 `q_*`-coherence chain. -/
783	private noncomputable def pushPullCoprodLegIso {C : Scheme.{u}} (q : (A ⨿ B) ⟶ X)
784	    (c : C ⟶ A ⨿ B) [IsOpenImmersion c] (pC : C ⟶ X) (wC : c ≫ q = pC) (F : X.Modules) :
785	    (pushforward q).obj ((pushforward c).obj
786	        (((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F).restrict c)) ≅
787	      pushPullObj F (Over.mk pC) :=
788	  (pushforward q).mapIso ((pushforward c).mapIso
789	    ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback c).app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F) ≪≫
790	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp c q).app F ≪≫
791	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr wC).app F)) ≪≫
792	  eqToIso (congrArg (fun p => (pushforward p).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback pC).obj F)) wC)

794	-- The final `congr 1` discharges the proof-irrelevant `eqToHom` over-triangle transports against
795	-- concrete pushforward/pullback objects, whose `whnf` exceeds the default heartbeat budget.
796	set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in
797	/-- (★) Per-leg coherence: the push–pull map of the over-inclusion `Over.homMk c : Over.mk pC ⟶
798	Over.mk q` is, through the canonical leg iso, the pushforward of the disjoint-cover restriction unit
799	`(restrictAdjunction c).unit`.  This is the bridge that converts the canonical comparison map
800	`prod.lift (pushPullMap F …)` into the manifestly-iso `coprodDecompMap` chain.  Project-local. -/
801	private lemma pushPull_binary_leg_coherence {C : Scheme.{u}} (q : (A ⨿ B) ⟶ X)
802	    (c : C ⟶ A ⨿ B) [IsOpenImmersion c] (pC : C ⟶ X) (wC : c ≫ q = pC) (F : X.Modules) :
803	    pushPullMap F (Over.homMk c wC : Over.mk pC ⟶ Over.mk q) =
804	      (pushforward q).map
805	          ((Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction c).unit.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) ≫
806	        (pushPullCoprodLegIso q c pC wC F).hom := by
807	  have hraw : pushPullMap F (Over.homMk c wC : Over.mk pC ⟶ Over.mk q)
808	      = rawPushPullMap c q pC wC F := rfl
809	  rw [hraw, rawPushPullMap_self_gen]
810	  have hLAU : (Scheme.Modules.restrictAdjunction c).unit.app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F) ≫
811	        (pushforward c).map
812	          ((Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback c).hom.app
813	            ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F)) =
814	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction c).unit.app
815	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F) :=
816	    Adjunction.unit_leftAdjointUniq_hom_app _ _ _
817	  subst wC
818	  simp only [pushPullCoprodLegIso, Iso.trans_hom, Functor.mapIso_hom, eqToIso.hom,
819	    Iso.app_hom, Category.comp_id,
820	    Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr, eqToIso_refl, Iso.refl_hom, NatTrans.id_app]
821	  rw [← hLAU]
822	  simp only [Functor.map_comp, Category.assoc]; rfl

862	(`backbone_hom_ext`), where both sides become canonical component maps. -/

866	      interLegHom 𝒰 σ' k ≫ backboneIncl 𝒰 p (σ' ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ k).toOrderHom) := by

874	/-- The section-at-`V` functor `Γ(V, ·) : X.Modules ⥤ Ab` (the `E` of
875	`pushPull_eval_prod_iso`). -/
876	noncomputable abbrev sectionFunctorV (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) : X.Modules ⥤ Ab.{u} :=
877	  Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X ⋙
878	    (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ Ab.{u}).obj (Opposite.op V)

881	morphisms of `X.Modules` (definitional: `restrictScalars (𝟙 _)` does not change the
882	underlying abelian presheaf map). -/
883	lemma GVΨ_map_eq (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) {M N : X.Modules} (φ : M ⟶ N) :
884	    (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
885	        (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op V)).map
886	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
887	          PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).map φ) =
888	    (sectionFunctorV V).map φ :=
889	  rfl

893	@[reassoc]
894	private lemma piMapIso_hom_π {β : Type u} {f g : β → Ab.{u}}
895	    (w : ∀ b, f b ≅ g b) (b : β) :
896	    (Pi.mapIso w).hom ≫ Pi.π g b = Pi.π f b ≫ (w b).hom := by
897	  have h := limMap_π (F := Discrete.functor f) (G := Discrete.functor g)
898	    (Discrete.natIso (fun j => w j.as)).hom ⟨b⟩
